'''Luis Roberts''' (born 24 March 2002) is a [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales international]] [[rugby league]] [[footballer]] who plays as a {{rlp|ce}} or {{rlp|wg}}er for the [[Midlands Hurricanes]] in the [[RFL Championship]].

He previously played for  [[Salford Red Devils]] and [[Leeds Rhinos]]  in the [[Super League]]; and  [[Swinton Lions]], [[Leigh Centurions]], [[Widnes Vikings]],  and [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[RFL Championship]].

==Background==
Roberts is of Welsh heritage and has featured for [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] at both junior and senior level.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.salfordreddevils.net/academy-luis-roberts-and-cian-rhys-selected-for-wales-u16s/ |title=Luis Roberts and Cian Rhys selected for Wales U16s |publisher=Salford Red Devils |date=26 June 2018}}</ref>

==Career==
===Salford Red Devils===

Roberts made his Super League debut in round 14 of the [[Super League XXV results|2020 Super League season]] for [[Salford Red Devils]] against the [[Warrington Wolves]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/salford-vs-warrington/teams/54568|title=Starting Lineups - Salford vs Warrington|publisher=Sky Sports |date=29 September 2020}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/54329439 |title=Super League: Salford Red Devils 20-18 Warrington Wolves |publisher=BBC Sport |date=29 September 2020}}</ref>
Roberts also featured in a one-off match for new expansion club 
Valencia Hurricanes in a loss to [[Featherstone Rovers]].

===Swinton Lions===
On 10 September 2020, it was announced that Roberts would join [[Swinton Lions]] for the 2021 season in the RFL Championship. He was nominated for the 2021 Championship Young  Player of the Year award.<ref>{{cite web |title=Betfred Championship and League 1 Awards | Nominees Announced |url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36060/betfred-championship-and-league-1-awards-%7C-nominees-announced |publisher=The Rugby Football League |access-date=29 October 2022 |date=22 September 2021}}</ref>

===Leigh Centurions===
On 28 October 2021, it was reported that he had signed for  [[Leigh Centurions]] in the 2022 RFL Championship.<ref>{{cite web|website=Leigh RL|date=28 Oct 2021|accessdate=29 Oct 2021|title=Luis Roberts Joins Leigh Centurions|url=https://leighrl.co.uk/wp/v2/blog/2021/10/28/luis-roberts-joins-leigh-centurions/}}</ref>

===Widnes Vikings (loan)===
On 23 June 2022, it was announced that he would join  [[Widnes Vikings]] on loan in the RFL Championship for the remainder of the 2022 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/widnes-kieran-dixon-luis-roberts-leigh|title=Widnes sign pair on loan from Leigh|website=www.loverugbyleague.com|date=23 June 2022 |accessdate=27 Jun 2022}}</ref>

===Leeds Rhinos===
On 18 October 2022, Roberts joined Super League side [[Leeds Rhinos]] for the 2023 season, signing a two-year contract.<ref>{{cite web |title=Luis Roberts: Leeds Rhinos sign Leigh Centurions outside back on two-year deal |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/63298300 |website=BBC Sport |access-date=29 October 2022 |date=18 October 2022}}</ref>
Roberts has also made a number of appearances for [[Bradford Bulls]] in the RFL championship on dual registration in 2023.

===Midlands Hurricanes===
On 21 Feb 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[Midlands Hurricanes]] in the [[RFL League 1]] on a 1-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=21 Feb 2025|author=Louis Chapman Coombe|title=Former Leeds Rhinos man Luis Roberts joins League 1 side after Headingley release|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/former-leeds-rhinos-man-luis-roberts-joins-league-1-side-after-headingley-release}}</ref>
On 18 July 2025 it was reported Roberts had signed a contract extension that will see him remain at the club until the end of the 2026 season in the RFL Championship.
